title: Subcontractor Access — Downstream API Keys Touching ePHI Interfaces

compliance.assurance_rationale: Auto-migrated from cat-22 markdown on Phase 1.3 run. Requires SME review to confirm clause accuracy, mode (satisfies vs detects-violation-of), and assurance level (contributing/partial/full).

dataSources: `index=interop` `sourcetype=apigee:proxy` or AWS API Gateway logs (`client_id`, `api_key`, `consumer`), `lookup ba_subcontractor_clients.csv`
app: HTTP Event Collector (HEC), Splunk Enterprise Security (263)
spl:
index=interop sourcetype="apigee:proxy" uri_path="/fhir/*" earliest=-30d
| stats earliest(_time) as first_seen latest(_time) as last_seen dc(status) as status_codes by client_id, api_key
| eval key_or_client=coalesce(client_id, api_key)
| lookup ba_subcontractor_clients.csv key_or_client OUTPUT approved_vendor, subcontractor_name
| where isnull(approved_vendor)
| sort first_seen
value: Monitors first-seen API keys or OAuth clients used by BA subcontractors against an approved key registry so the extended BAA chain does not introduce unreviewed ePHI paths.
implementation: (1) Hash static API keys at ingest with `sha256(api_key)` for display fields; (2) require procurement to register subcontractor keys before production; (3) auto-revoke via API management integration on critical alerts.
visualization: Table (first_seen, key hash, path), Timeline (new clients per week).
knownFalsePositives: Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
